climate.description: Temperate Irish summer with variable weather; expect clouds, possible showers, mild temps, and coastal breezes.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: A scenic loop showcasing County Clare's rugged cliffs, rolling hills, quiet country lanes, and Shannon Estuary views; festive atmosphere with challenging yet rewarding climbs.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Hand climb combined with coastal winds and estuary rollers, blending steep efforts with endurance-testing scenery.
suffering_zones: The Hand: Steep wall-like climb outside Kilmaley; short but punchy, rewarding with panoramic hill views and fast descent.
Kilkee Cliffs Loop: Breathtaking coastal roads with rugged beauty; undulating terrain tests pacing amid stunning ocean vistas.
Shannon Estuary Rollers: Deceptively tough final stretch with headwinds, rolling hills, and estuary views demanding grit to the finish.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Ennis offers hotels, B&Bs, and guesthouses; book early for July event; camping options nearby.
logistics.transport: Hosted in Ennis town center; two routes (160km and 100km); starts with DJ hype, multiple food stops, medal finish, and post-ride BBQ.

history.origin_story: Annual sportive celebrating County Clare's scenery; known for community spirit, quality support, and as a summer highlight.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ireland's most scenic and festive road sportive: cliffs, cakes, and community magic.
